Label: Window Title
Notes: Floating window launched from SystemConfigTab's "Audio Interface and Patching Window" button. Live-applies changes on the fly; no Save/Cancel buttons (there is no modal save/revert flow).
Hover help text in the status bar: Audio interface selection, hardware patching, and test-signal generation.

Label: Current Device Type
Notes: Read-only. Shows the current audio device type (ASIO / DirectSound / CoreAudio etc.) or "No Device" when none is selected. Updates via 1 Hz timer.
Hover help text in the status bar: Current audio device type.

Label: Current Device Name
Notes: Read-only. Shows the current audio device name or "Not configured". Updates via 1 Hz timer.
Hover help text in the status bar: Current audio device.

Label: Current Sample Rate
Notes: Read-only. Shows the active sample rate in Hz. Updates via 1 Hz timer.
Hover help text in the status bar: Current sample rate.

Label: Current Buffer Size
Notes: Read-only. Shows the active audio buffer size in samples. Updates via 1 Hz timer.
Hover help text in the status bar: Current audio buffer size.

Label: Audio Device Type
Notes: Enum populated from JUCE AudioDeviceManager::getAvailableDeviceTypes(). Platform-dependent list. Changing the type repopulates the Device combobox below. Persisted by AppSettings (application-level, not project-level).
Hover help text in the status bar: Select the audio device type (driver).

Label: Device
enum: (dynamic: hardware devices for the selected type)
Notes: Enum populated from the selected device type's available devices. All device channels are auto-enabled on selection. Persisted by AppSettings.
Hover help text in the status bar: Select the audio interface.

Label: Sample Rate
enum: (dynamic: rates supported by the selected device)
Notes: Enum populated from the device's supported sample rates. Persisted by AppSettings.
Hover help text in the status bar: Audio sample rate.

Label: Buffer Size
enum: (dynamic: buffer sizes supported by the selected device)
Notes: Enum populated from the device's supported buffer sizes. Persisted by AppSettings.
Hover help text in the status bar: Audio buffer size in samples.

Label: Control Panel
Notes: Calls AudioIODevice::showControlPanel() on the current device. Primarily useful for ASIO drivers. Button is inactive for device types that don't expose a control panel.
Hover help text in the status bar: Open the audio driver's native control panel (if available).

Label: Reset Device
Notes: Closes and reopens the current device with a 100 ms gap. Used to recover stuck ASIO drivers or re-pick up device changes made externally.
Hover help text in the status bar: Close and reopen the current audio device to recover from driver errors.

Label: Signal Flow Diagram
Notes: Static SVG showing the system's signal flow. A "You are here" animated pin icon highlights the WFS processor stage. No user interaction. Re-renders on color-scheme change.
Hover help text in the status bar: Signal-flow diagram (read-only).

Label: Tab Selector
Notes: Two sub-tabs. Changing tabs resets the current sub-tab's interaction mode back to Scrolling and stops any running test signal.
Hover help text in the status bar: Select the Input Patch or Output Patch sub-tab.

Label: Mode
Notes: Two-way radio group (RadioGroupId=1). Defaults to Scrolling when the tab is entered and when the window is closed. No Testing mode on the Input tab (Testing applies only to outputs).
Hover help text in the status bar: Matrix interaction mode: drag to scroll, or click cells to patch/unpatch.

Label: Unpatch All
Notes: Long-press to confirm. Clears the entire Input Patch matrix.
Hover help text in the status bar: Long-press to clear all input patches.

Label: Input Matrix
Notes: Stored in ValueTree under AudioPatch > InputPatch > patchData as "r1c1,r1c2,...;r2c1,r2c2,..." (rows separated by ';', cells by ','; '1' patched, '0' unpatched). Rows = WFS Input Channels, Columns = hardware inputs. Constraint: each WFS input maps to <= 1 hardware channel and vice versa. Diagonal left-drag patches a diagonal run; clicking an already-patched cell unpatches it.
Hover help text in the status bar: Input patch matrix. Scroll in Scrolling mode; click/drag to patch in Patching mode.
Keyboard shortcuts: Arrow keys move selected cell; Enter/Space toggles patch in Patching mode.

Label: Input Patch Rows
Notes: ValueTree int under AudioPatch > InputPatch > rows. Row count of the Input patch matrix. Mirrors the System Config > Input Channels value.
Hover help text in the status bar: Number of WFS input rows in the patch matrix.

Label: Input Patch Cols
Notes: ValueTree int under AudioPatch > InputPatch > cols. Column count (typically 64, capped at device channel count).
Hover help text in the status bar: Number of hardware input columns in the patch matrix.

Label: Active Hardware Inputs
Notes: ValueTree int under AudioPatch > InputPatch > activeHardwareInputs. Hardware inputs beyond this count are drawn dimmed and cannot be patched (but existing patches on them render as orange row headers).
Hover help text in the status bar: Number of hardware inputs currently active on the selected device.

Label: Input Header Peak Tint
Notes: Column headers are tinted live from hardwareInputPeakProvider at 20 Hz. Not a user control - purely visual feedback.
Hover help text in the status bar: Live peak indication on hardware input column headers.

Label: Mode
Notes: Three-way radio group (RadioGroupId=2). Defaults to Scrolling when the tab is entered and when the window is closed. Testing mode reveals the Test Signal panel below and changes left-click to "play test signal on this hardware channel" instead of patch edit.
Hover help text in the status bar: Matrix interaction mode: scroll, patch edit, or play test signals into individual outputs.

Label: Unpatch All
Notes: Long-press to confirm. Clears the entire Output Patch matrix.
Hover help text in the status bar: Long-press to clear all output patches.

Label: Output Matrix
Notes: Stored in ValueTree under AudioPatch > OutputPatch > patchData (same format as InputPatch). Rows = WFS Output Channels, Columns = hardware outputs. In Testing mode, clicking a cell plays the current test signal through that hardware output (highlighted green). First 2 output columns get a headphone icon when binaural monitoring is enabled in System Config.
Hover help text in the status bar: Output patch matrix. Click/drag to patch in Patching mode, click cells to test in Testing mode.
Keyboard shortcuts: Arrow keys move selected cell; Enter/Space toggles patch or test trigger.

Label: Output Patch Rows
Notes: ValueTree int under AudioPatch > OutputPatch > rows. Row count of the Output patch matrix. Mirrors the System Config > Output Channels value.
Hover help text in the status bar: Number of WFS output rows in the patch matrix.

Label: Output Patch Cols
Notes: ValueTree int under AudioPatch > OutputPatch > cols. Column count (typically 64, capped at device channel count).
Hover help text in the status bar: Number of hardware output columns in the patch matrix.

Label: Active Hardware Outputs
Notes: ValueTree int under AudioPatch > OutputPatch > activeHardwareOutputs. Columns beyond this count are dimmed.
Hover help text in the status bar: Number of hardware outputs currently active on the selected device.

Label: Binaural Output Marker
Notes: Headphone icon drawn over the first 2 output columns when binaural solo monitoring is enabled in System Config. Not a user control.
Hover help text in the status bar: Binaural monitoring output pair (visual marker).

Label: Signal Type
enum: Off (0) ; Pink Noise (1) ; Tone (2) ; Sweep (3) ; Dirac Pulse (4)
Notes: Not persisted (lives in the TestSignalGenerator DSP class). Visible only when Output Patch mode = Testing. Selecting Off stops any active test signal, clears the matrix highlight, and turns Hold OFF. Selecting a non-Off type auto-boosts Level to -40 dB if it was below -80 dB to prevent inaudible tests. Enum values: Off, PinkNoise, Tone, Sweep, DiracPulse from enum class SignalType in Source/DSP/TestSignalGenerator.h.
Hover help text in the status bar: Select the test signal to play when clicking a cell in Testing mode.

Label: Frequency
Notes: Visible only when Signal Type = Tone. Slider uses setSkewFactorFromMidPoint(1000.0) so the center of the range sits at 1 kHz. Not persisted.
Hover help text in the status bar: Tone frequency (20 Hz - 20 kHz, logarithmic skew).

Label: Level
Notes: Step size 0.1 dB. Editable label supports direct typing. Default -40 dB. 500 ms linear fade-in applied when the signal starts (for Pink Noise and Tone) to prevent loud bursts. Not persisted.
Hover help text in the status bar: Test signal level (dB).

Label: Hold
Notes: When ON, the test signal continues playing after releasing the mouse click on a cell, until Hold is turned OFF or another cell is clicked. Turning OFF stops the signal and clears the matrix highlight. Not persisted.
Hover help text in the status bar: Keep the test signal playing after releasing the mouse.

Label: Active Test Output
Notes: Atomic int in TestSignalGenerator tracking which hardware output channel is currently receiving the test signal. -1 = no active channel. Set by clicking a cell in Testing mode. Not persisted, not directly user-editable.
Hover help text in the status bar: Hardware output channel currently receiving the test signal.

Label: Pink Noise
Notes: Continuous Gardner-method 7-octave filtered pink noise. 500 ms linear fade-in when signal starts.
Hover help text in the status bar: Continuous pink-noise generator with fade-in.

Label: Tone
Notes: Sine oscillator at the Frequency slider value. 500 ms linear fade-in when signal starts.
Hover help text in the status bar: Sine-tone generator with fade-in.

Label: Sweep
Notes: 20 Hz -> 20 kHz logarithmic sweep over 1 s, 3 s silent gap, repeats. No user-adjustable parameters.
Hover help text in the status bar: Repeating 20 Hz - 20 kHz log sweep, 1 s sweep, 3 s gap.

Label: Dirac Pulse
Notes: 5 ms burst at 2.0x amplitude every 1 s. No user-adjustable parameters. Useful for latency measurement.
Hover help text in the status bar: Repeating single-sample impulse (every 1 s).

Label: Left-drag diagonal (Patching)
Notes: In Patching mode, dragging diagonally from cell A to cell B patches a sequential 1:1 run of cells along the diagonal. Convenient for patching N -> N mappings in one sweep.
Hover help text in the status bar: Diagonal drag patches a 1:1 sequential run.

Label: Right-drag / 3-finger-drag
Notes: Scrolls the matrix viewport at any time regardless of the selected Mode. Useful for navigating large matrices without leaving Patching or Testing mode.
Hover help text in the status bar: Pan the matrix viewport without leaving the current mode.

Label: Single-click (Patching)
Notes: Click an unpatched cell to patch it. Click an already-patched cell to unpatch it. Enforces the 1:1 constraint: patching cell (r,c) automatically unpatches any existing patch in row r or column c.
Hover help text in the status bar: Click to patch or unpatch a single cell.

Label: Single-click (Testing)
Notes: Click any cell to route the current test signal to that hardware output. Highlight turns green. If Hold is OFF the signal stops on mouse release; if Hold is ON the signal keeps playing until another cell is clicked or Signal Type is set to Off.
Hover help text in the status bar: Click to play test signal on this hardware output.

Label: Keyboard navigation
Notes: Arrow keys move the selected cell. Enter / Space activates the selected cell (patch toggle in Patching, test trigger in Testing). Automatically scrolls to keep the selected cell visible.
Hover help text in the status bar: Arrow keys to navigate cells, Enter/Space to activate.

Label: Horizontal Scrollbar
Notes: Bottom scrollbar that moves the column viewport. Thickness scales with UI scale factor.
Hover help text in the status bar: Scroll columns.

Label: Vertical Scrollbar
Notes: Right scrollbar that moves the row viewport. Thickness scales with UI scale factor.
Hover help text in the status bar: Scroll rows.
